channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: DKV más que triplica su resultado neto de 2025 tras la salida de Muface
item.description: Han sido, según presentó ayer el director general financiero, Javier Cubría, los mejores resultados en la historia de la compañía, en un año como 2025 que fue “extraordinario” por la salida del concierto de Muface. En relación a Muface, el directivo confirmó que se han interpuesto sendos recursos ante juzgados de lo Contencioso-Administrativ o en los que la entidad reclama una compensación por las pérdidas que, sostiene, se produjeron por la insuficiencia de las primas fijadas en conjunto del concierto 2022-2024, por un lado, y en la prórroga forzosa que el Gobierno impuso durante los cinco primeros meses de 2025. DKV Seguros logró el pasado año un beneficio neto atribuido de 54,6 millones de euros, cifra que supone más que triplicar los 14,7 millones del ejercicio precedente.

item.title: Así estamos repensando la protección empresarial
item.description: Por eso, en Generali hemos dado un paso que para mí es especialmente importante: hemos creado una unidad nueva dirección comercial especializada en Empresas , un equipo que vive el terreno, que entiende las particularidades de cada actividad y que trabaja con una convicción muy sencilla: la protección tiene que ser útil, cercana y humana. Hoy ofrecemos una nueva propuesta de valor para todo el proceso, con una suscripción más ágil y con una propuesta de productos modular, flexible y fácil de entender , que permite que cada negocio configure su protección a medida, sin comprar más de lo que necesita ni quedarse corto en lo esencial. En definitiva, nuestra aspiración y nuestro compromiso no es otro que ser una compañía cercana y referente en el aseguramiento del tejido empresarial Español, en ello vamos a trabajar todo el equipo en todo el territorio.

item.title: Accede a las cifras de 110 aseguradoras españolas
item.description: INESE Data publica ‘Las aseguradoras españolas en cifras 2025’ . Este estudio permite acceder a las cuentas técnicas por ramos, balances, cuentas no técnicas, fondos propios y diversos ratios de siniestralidad, gastos y resultados de 2025 de 110 aseguradoras españolas , integradas en 67 grupos, con una cuota de mercado del 95,63%. El estudio permite conocer cómo han evolucionado las aseguradoras en estos parámetros, ya que los compara con la información anual definitiva presentada a la DGSFP correspondiente a 2024.

item.title: Adjusters: Why the Indemnification Clause Should Stay Top of Mind
item.description: It materializes months later, when the claims professional finally evaluates the clause and finds the carrier absorbing defense costs it was never obligated to pay or honoring indemnification demands that were never enforceable. On files with that level of exposure, the indemnification clause is not a background document. Treat the indemnification clause as an open item from the moment it appears in the file until it has been fully evaluated.

item.title: Purdue’s $5.5B Sentencing for Opioid Charges Delayed After Victims Show at Court
item.description: Related: Purdue Pharma Gets Court Nod for Bankruptcy Exit, Sackler Deal Arleo initially scheduled the hearing to occur on Zoom, but changed course during the proceedings and delayed it until April 28 after protesters and members of the public showed up at court to participate in the sentencing hearing. A federal judge on Tuesday delayed the planned criminal sentencing of Purdue Pharma by one week, saying she wanted to allow more public participation before concluding a criminal case over the company’s marketing of addictive opioid drugs. District Judge Madeline Cox Arleo was expected to accept Purdue’s 2020 guilty plea at a court hearing in Newark, New Jersey, which would have imposed a $3.5 billion criminal fine and $2 billion in criminal forfeiture against the company for its role in fueling an opioid epidemic that has claimed more than 1 million lives in the U.S.

item.title: Microsoft Must Face $2.8B UK Lawsuit Over Cloud Computing Licenses
item.description: Microsoft must face a mass lawsuit alleging it overcharged thousands of British businesses to use Windows Server software on cloud computing services provided by Amazon, Google and Alibaba, a London tribunal ruled on Tuesday. are separately examining the practices of Microsoft and other firms in cloud computing. They argued at a hearing last year that the businesses were overcharged because Microsoft charges higher wholesale prices for Windows Server than for users of Azure, costs that are passed on to customers and make Azure cheaper than Amazon’s AWS or Google Cloud.

item.title: General average declared after Singapore containership fire
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Japan on high alert for second earthquake after first brings tsunamis, minor damage, injuries
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Newline promotes Rebecca Smith to senior underwriter for Australia PI team
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Aon appoints Subhash Khanna as Director, Life Solutions, Reinsurance
item.description: Aon’s Reinsurance Solutions, the reinsurance division of the global broking group, has appointed Subhash Khanna as a Director of the Life Solutions team within its Strategy and Technology Group, based in London. In the past, Khanna also served as a Senior Risk Analyst for Risk Management at McKinsey & Company, and as an Assistant Vice President at Swiss Re. Most recently, he served at Milliman for over 11 years, as Principal & Consulting Actuary and Consulting Actuary, based in both London and India.

item.title: Cowbell introduces Prime One cyber insurance product in the United States
item.description: Cowbell, an insurance technology company that provides adaptive cyber and specialty insurance solutions to small and medium-sized organisations globally, has announced the launch of Cowbell Prime One in the United States. The new product is a non-admitted cyber insurance solution created for organisations with significant digital risk exposure and annual revenues between $250 million and $1 billion. The US launch builds on Cowbell’s experience in international markets, where collaboration with global specialty re/insurance provider Chaucer has supported the development of cyber insurance solutions tailored to complex risk environments.

item.title: Allianz Re hires Dr. Jonas Haug from Munich Re as Global Head of Group Ceded Reinsurance
item.description: Jonas Haug is set to join Allianz Reinsurance as Global Head of Group Ceded Reinsurance, tasked with driving the development of the firm’s reinsurance strategy in line with the Group’s risk profile. Haug will lead Allianz Re’s Group Ceded Reinsurance team from July 1st, 2026, succeeding Sang Hun Park, who has held the role since May 2023. Haug joined Munich Re in 2015 as Project Manager Corporate Strategy/Reinsurance Strategy, and in 2019 was appointed Global Business Developer/Underwriter – CIP Property at the European reinsurer.

item.title: MS High Court Weighs in on Woman Taken for Organ Donation But Was Still Alive
item.description: Jaques declined to comment while the case is still pending, but his appeal brief argued that the organ recover agency performed surgery on the living woman without anesthesia, something the immunity law does not apply to. Within a few hours, the woman was pronounced “brain dead” by a physician, then “legally dead.” Denison’s family arranged for her to be transported to the Mississippi Organ Recovery Agency, or MORA, a nonprofit center in Flowood, about an hour away, where her organs could be recovered and donated to patients in need. The woman was taken back to the hospital, where, a day later, she was pronounced dead—again.

item.title: Claim Severity Trends for Medical Malpractice ‘Stand Out’: S&P GMI
item.description: According to an S&P Global Market Intelligence report, written by Husain Rupawala, Tim Zawacki and Jason Woleben, medical professional liability “stands out as the most severity-pressured line in casualty insurance, with the highest figure across all lines.” While severity steps down as claims mature and paid amounts reduce outstanding balances, the authors note such “moderation in newer years” primarily reflects claim immaturity rather than genuine improvement in underlying loss costs. Medical professional liability occurrence reached $151,768 per open claim in accident year 2018, which represents the highest severity figure across all casualty insurance lines before moderating in more recent years, a typical trend for newer claims where cost pressures continue to emerge and mature. Medical professional liability claim severity varies significantly by state due to legal environments and the presence or absence of caps on noneconomic damages with New Mexico being one of the most difficult markets, the report noted.

item.title: What AI-Ready Insurance APIs Actually Look Like (And How to Start Using Them)
item.description: What AI-Ready Insurance APIs Actually Look Like (And How to Start Using Them) April 2026 Eight minutes. That is how long it took to build a working insurance application using AI coding against InsureMO APIs. This is exactly what’s happening with AI and insurance APIs right now.

item.title: State Farm Paid a ‘Hail’ of a Lot of Claims in 2025
item.description: Providing its annual report of hail-related claims , State Farm said that it paid out $5.6 billion nationally for such claims in 2025, and $1.4 billion in Texas alone. Rounding out the top three states, State Farm paid 10% more claim dollars for hail-related damage in 2025 than 2024 for the state of Missouri, which now has the second-highest total, while paying 28% less for hail-related claims in Illinois. The states with the lowest level of hail claim payouts on the 2024 top 10 list—Iowa and South Carolina—each made that list with $109 million in claims paid by State Farm for hail.

item.title: Carlos Grijalva to join Gallagher Re as APAC head of cyber
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: ‘We’re just getting started’: CyberCube bets on Asia’s cyber insurance gap as the ‘next big frontier’
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Amish Mother and 6 Children Killed in Explosion and Fire at Pennsylvania Home
item.description: An Amish woman and her six children ranging in age from 3 to 11 were killed in a swift-moving house fire after an explosion that shook nearby houses in rural northcentral Pennsylvania, authorities said. Firefighters responding to a report of an explosion and fire at a home in Lamar Township in Clinton County near Mill Hall on Sunday morning said seven people were trapped, but they couldn’t search the house engulfed in flames, Pennsylvania State Police said in a statement. A propane leak inside the home may have caused the explosion and fire, police said, noting that exterior propane tanks did not explode and were not contributing factors for the explosion and fire.

item.title: How One Agency Achieved 50% Year-Over-Year Financing Growth
item.description: Tongue saw their financing volume jump by 50% year-over-year . Now, instead of a separate, multi-step process, financing is a natural, digital part of the checkout flow. The ability for all of that information to feed directly over is key.” — Sarah Goldbach , R.K.

item.title: iPipeline FIDx Launch IX Express Solution
item.description: iPipeline (Exton, Pa.) and Fiduciary Exchange LLC (FIDx, Berwyn, Pa.) have launched IX Express, a digital annuity distribution solution designed to expand access for small and mid-market broker-dealers and financial institutions. The platform integrates iPipeline’s AFFIRM annuity technology with FIDx’s Insurance Exchange to provide a unified system for annuity processing. The companies say the solution is intended to reduce operational complexity and support digital adoption among firms with limited resources.

item.title: AI Echoes Past Tech Debt Cycles in Insurance
item.description: George Santayana is often credited with observing that “those who cannot remember the past are condemned to repeat it.” The point holds. The resulting technical debt took years to unwind across the industry. There’s little doubt that we live in an exciting and vibrant time from a technological perspective.

item.title: Uber Loses Another US Driver Sex Assault Trial
item.description: Related: Uber Faces Second Driver Sexual Assault Trial Following $8.5M Verdict Although Monday’s award is relatively small, it further establishes that jurors may not hesitate to find Uber liable for assaults by drivers in more of the 3,300 similar cases consolidated in federal court. A federal jury in North Carolina on Monday said Uber should pay $5,000 to a woman who claimed she was sexually assaulted by a driver she booked through the app, as the company faces thousands of similar claims. It comes after a separate jury in Arizona earlier this year awarded $8.5 million to a woman who claimed an Uber driver had raped her when she was 19.

item.title: Three Sentenced in Bear-Suit Attacks Insurance Fraud Case
item.description: An insurance fraud scheme involving a bear costume, luxury vehicles and falsified damage claims has ended in convictions for three Los Angeles area residents. The CDI investigation discovered the defendants used a person in a bear suit to stage fake attacks on high-end vehicles, then submitted fraudulent claims seeking payouts from insurance companies. The CDI had a biologist from the California Department of Fish and Wildlife review the three alleged bear videos and the expert verified it was a human in a bear suit.

item.title: Exclusive: Schematic Raises $6.5M To Help Companies Update Their Pricing Faster And Easier In The AI Era
item.description: By using Schematic, Glover said that companies like Plotly went from taking weeks to change their pricing to just 10 minutes. Schematic , a startup that aims to simplify pricing and packaging for software and AI companies, has raised $6.5 million in seed funding, it tells Crunchbase News exclusively. Schematic builds entitlements and enforcement infrastructure for SaaS and AI companies.

item.title: At least $115bn to $125bn of cat losses needed to shift property pricing trajectory: Gallagher Re
item.description: Leading Gallagher Re to explain, “We now estimate that it would require a single event (or series of large events) resulting in an insured loss of at least USD115 billion to USD125 billion — above expected average annual catastrophe losses — to meaningfully impact the trajectory of pricing in the property sector of the industry.” In order for the current downward trajectory of pricing in property insurance and reinsurance to be meaningfully shifted broker Gallagher Re says that an anomalously high loss burden will be required. Adding that, “Even given the continuing softening we saw at the January 1 and April 1 reinsurance renewals (with property cat pricing down 15% to 25%), it will require an anomalously high loss performance to meaningfully shift pricing behavior.” In fact, catastrophe losses have been manageable for the global insurance and reinsurance industry for a number of quarters now.

item.title: Fuse unveils Watch, a live peril dashboard for commercial insurance
item.description: Fuse International, an AI-powered commercial insurance intelligence platform, has announced the general availability of Watch, a new product that plots every active US peril on a single live map, purpose-built for commercial insurance brokers and underwriters. According to the announcement, Watch is organised around four views: a Peril Map plotting active events across the continental United States, a Live Feed of insurance-relevant alerts with line-of-business context, a Forecast covering the next three days of severe-weather risk, as well as Market Pulse, a live read on the macro and commodity signals that drive insurance pricing. It’s a live peril dashboard every commercial underwriter and every broker should have open on a second monitor.

item.title: Tower Hill secures 67% upsized $375m Winston Re 2026-1 cat bond at lower pricing
item.description: Tower Hill Insurance Exchange has now successfully priced its latest catastrophe bond to secure the two-third upsized $375 million of fully-collateralized reinsurance protection against named storm losses in Florida from the Winston Re Ltd. We are now told that Tower Hill Insurance Exchange has secured the upper-end of that target, with the notes now all priced and this Winston Re Series 2026-1 catastrophe bond set to provide the insurer with $375 million of fully-collateralized reinsurance protection. Tower Hill made its return to the catastrophe bond market at the end of March, initially seeking $225 million of fully-collateralized reinsurance protection against named storm losses in Florida .

item.title: Blenheim Underwriting adds Munich Re’s Ian Ritchie as Contingency Underwriter
item.description: Blenheim Underwriting, a specialist managing general agent (MGA) and Lloyd’s coverholder, has appointed Ian Ritchie as Contingency Underwriter, effective June 2026. With this appointment, Ritchie joins Blenheim Syndicate 5886 non-appearance contingency team, led by Justin Burns, working alongside the existing Contingency Underwriter, Jane Todd. Ritchie has over two decades of experience in the contingency market, bringing with him a wealth of knowledge from both a claims and underwriting background.

item.title: Boleron says it is first insurance broker approved by OpenAI to distribute through ChatGPT
item.description: Bulgaria-based Boleron has received approval from OpenAI to distribute insurance products directly through ChatGPT, reportedly making it the first licensed insurance broker in the world authorised for AI-agent distribution, and the only one of four approved insurance companies globally operating with multi-jurisdictional regulatory coverage. And we were the first in the world to do it through a regulated broker model — the model AI platforms will increasingly require for European scale.” Boleron’s announcement comes just a few months after the first OpenAI-related insurance approvals triggered a major shift in global broker stocks. The four insurance companies approved by OpenAI for ChatGPT distribution now include Tuio (Spain) , Insurify (United States), BizCover (Australia) and Boleron.

item.title: Lloyd’s delivers strong results despite softer pricing: Fitch
item.description: Fitch concludes that the Lloyd’s market’s disciplined approach to underwriting, reserving, and investment management leaves it well placed to navigate a softer pricing cycle over the next two years. Fitch expects this downward trend to become more pronounced in 2026, although it stresses that pricing remains at a level that should continue to support solid underwriting results despite broader economic and geopolitical uncertainty. The global credit rating agency highlights that Lloyd’s continues to hold a very strong capital position and expects its credit profile to remain robust through 2026, even as pricing conditions begin to weaken and claims linked to the Iran conflict emerge.

item.title: Texas Speaker Seeks to Investigate Roblox Over Game Simulating Uvalde Shooting
item.description: Texas House Speaker Dustin Burrows on Monday targeted Roblox for alleged child endangerment, after learning the platform offered a simulation of the 2022 Uvalde mass shooting. Don McLaughlin, who represents Uvalde and was mayor of the city at the time of the shooting, thanked Burrows for “recognizing the seriousness” of child safety in a statement on Monday and condemned the Roblox simulation of the 2022 shooting. In a news release, he call Roblox a place where “exploitative content and nominal safeguards are exposing Texas children to ongoing endangerment.” The charge adds to a list of policy priorities Burrows has focused the House around ahead of next year’s legislative session.

item.title: Alabama Governor Signs New Captive Law That Could Help End DOI Moratorium
item.description: Alabama has a new law governing captive insurance companies, and supporters hope it will mean that state regulators will soon lift a moratorium on new captives in the state. “The law takes effect in June, so we’re hoping that maybe by the end of summer the moratorium will be ended,” said Travis Bowden, president of Crossroads Risk Management, a captive management firm active in Alabama and Georgia. A spokesperson for the Alabama Department of Insurance said Tuesday: “Now that the act has been signed into law by Governor Ivey, the department is working to determine the best timing for bringing the moratorium to an end.” The law, the result of Alabama House Bill 415, was signed by Gov.

item.title: Newell Succeeds Founder Eknoian as World Insurance CEO
item.description: Brokerage World Insurance Associates announced John Newell will become chief executive officer, succeeding founder and long-time CEO Rich Eknoian. World said Newell will build on its momentum by continuing to invest in top-tier talent, expanding vertical expertise and accelerating enterprise-wide AI enablement. Eknoian will assume the role of executive chairman, and will give counsel and support as well as stay involved in the team and culture he built since founding World in 2011 and growing it to be one of the country’s largest independent brokerages.

item.title: Allianz Türkiye adopts Nettle AI to transform commercial risk engineering
item.description: Allianz Türkiye has selected the Nettle AI platform to support its commercial property risk engineering operations, following a pilot programme that demonstrated significant efficiency gains in property inspections. During testing, Allianz Türkiye’s risk engineering teams were able to complete inspections up to three times faster, with structured outputs delivered more rapidly to underwriting teams. Ceyhun Eren, Director of Allianz Teknik & Risk Engineering at Allianz Türkiye, highlighted the platform’s impact on operational speed and its potential to reshape the future of risk engineering.

item.title: Tokio Marine & Nichido Fire Insurance deploys EIS ClaimPulse to digitise travel insurance claims
item.description: “By enabling digital FNOL for overseas travel insurance, TMNF is improving convenience for customers and creating a more scalable, resilient way to handle fluctuations in claim volumes, without compromising service quality.” The post Tokio Marine & Nichido Fire Insurance deploys EIS ClaimPulse to digitise travel insurance claims appeared first on Insurtech Insights . EIS, a leading AI-native core platform provider to the insurance industry, has announced that Tokio Marine & Nichido Fire Insurance (TMNF) has deployed ClaimPulse as part of its new overseas travel insurance offering. ClaimPulse enhances claims automation by enabling customers to initiate claims digitally and improves downstream claims processing through a self-service, omnichannel experience.

item.title: World Insurance Associates appoints John Newell as CEO to drive next phase of growth
item.description: The post World Insurance Associates appoints John Newell as CEO to drive next phase of growth appeared first on Insurtech Insights . World Insurance Associates has appointed John Newell as chief executive officer, as founder Rich Eknoian transitions to the role of executive chairman. At World, Newell will focus on driving organic growth, deepening industry specialisation, and advancing the firm’s use of technology, including AI tools to support brokers and client service teams.

item.title: Cowbell launches Prime One in the US
item.description: Cowbell has launched Cowbell Prime One in the US, a non-admitted cyber product targeting mid-market companies with revenues between $250 million and $1 billion. Prime One also includes coverage for potential encryption vulnerabilities tied to quantum computing, as well as risks stemming from unauthorized access to AI systems. The product also includes access to Cowbell’s risk assessment and cybersecurity training services, with additional incentives tied to its managed detection and response offering.

item.title: Staying Compliance-Ready with Automated Benefits Management
item.description: Their experience illustrates how agencies can not only manage risk but also build stronger client relationships by staying compliance-ready with the right tools. Insurance agencies serving increasingly complex clients must stay on top of evolving coverages, renewals, and benefits requirements – including compliance-critical details that can easily be overlooked when data is fragmented. Modern benefits management technology helps agencies centralize critical plan and client information, enabling them to respond quickly and accurately throughout the policy lifecycle.

item.title: Kemper announces sale of Newins distribution operation
item.description: Kemper announced it has completed the sale of a property and casualty distribution operation consisting of captive agents and storefront locations to insurance broker Confie. The transaction includes the sale of Newins Insurance Agency Holdings, LLC, which operates under the Illinois Vehicle, A-Abana, and Access Auto Insurance brands in a limited number of states. The operation was acquired by Kemper as part of the American Access Insurance Company transaction in 2021.

item.title: PCRIC makes voluntary US$550,000 payment to Fiji for Cyclone Vaianu recovery
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Abu Dhabi National Insurance Company gets Gift City licence
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Supporting fintech in the next phase of innovation
item.description: Speaker : Jessica Rusu, chief data, information and intelligence officer Event: IFGS, UK FinTech Week Delivered: 21 April 2026 Note: This is a drafted speech and may differ from the delivered version Reading time: 5 minutes Twenty-five years ago, e-commerce changed how we buy. UK Fintech contributes more than £11bn annually to the economy. Seventy-five per cent of UK adults use fintech services, and Open Banking has reached over 16 million users – a scale few markets globally can match.

item.title: FCA announces second cohort for AI Live Testing
item.description: Applications for the AI Live Testing second cohort opened in January 2026, with firms beginning testing in April. Speaking at UK FinTech Week, Jessica Rusu, chief data, information and intelligence officer at the FCA, has confirmed the second group of firms selected to join AI Live Testing. The FCA is working with its technical partner Advai, a London-based specialist in automated AI assurance, to provide AI Live Testing .

item.title: The New Unicorn Count Reached A 4-Year High In March, Led By Robotics, Frontier Labs And AI Infrastructure
item.description: AI-centric sectors by far led unicorn creation in March, with 14 of the 36 newcomers hailing from the robotics, foundational AI or AI infrastructure industries: The Crunchbase Unicorn Board is a curated list that includes private unicorn companies with post-money valuations of $1 billion or more and is based on Crunchbase data. A total of 37 companies joined The Crunchbase Unicorn Board in March, the highest monthly count in close to four years, Crunchbase data shows. Frontier labs added four new unicorns, including two that are building models for robotics.

item.title: The Counterintuitive Truth About Product Pricing
item.description: This highlights a counterintuitive truth in strategy: In real-world markets, free or lower prices don’t always drive demand. Same product, same value proposition, but now there is a monthly subscription. Conversely, premium pricing attracts partners who value reliability and performance.

item.title: Global insured catastrophe losses hit $20bn in Q1 2026: Gallagher Re
item.description: Gallagher Re’s report highlighted how during the first three months of 2026, there were several global natural catastrophe events that had significant societal impacts; however, these did not result in unusually high economic or insured losses. Reinsurance broker Gallagher Re has estimated that total insured catastrophe losses for the opening quarter of 2026 reached at least US$20 billion, 26% lower than the decadal average of $26 billion, and 47% below the most recent 5-year average. The broker’s Q1 2026 natural catastrophe and climate report also revealed that economic losses from all natural perils in Q1’26 reached at least $58 billion, around 12% below the 10-year Q1 average.

item.title: Eaton Vance mutual funds benefit from strong reinsurance sidecar and ILS returns
item.description: As we previously reported, three of the Eaton Vance mutual funds have been seen to contain ILS and reinsurance sidecar strategies. Eaton Vance, part of Morgan Stanley Investment Management, has benefitted from the strong return environment in insurance-linked securities and reinsurance sidecar investments, with its allocations seemingly all accruing positive value. We’ve reported before on three of the Eaton Vance mutual fund portfolio strategies that contain allocations to ILS and reinsurance sidecar strategies.

item.title: IoT sensors and satellite imagery to play key role in future of parametric flood insurance: Swiss Re
item.description: A new report from global reinsurer Swiss Re has outlined that an array of sensor technologies has transformed how flood risk can be measured, from Internet of Things (IoT) flood sensors and satellite imagery to remote sensing and high-resolution flood models, all of which can play a key role in the future of parametric flood insurance. Adoption of parametric solutions has been supported by technological advances including granular flood mapping tools, remote sensing, satellite imagery and Internet of Things (IoT) sensors, to more precisely assess flood risks and accurately determine when catastrophic events trigger policyholder payments,” Swiss Re’s report explained. Additionally, Swiss Re also acknowledges that parametric flood insurance was historically limited in terms of data availability and accuracy.

item.title: Fuse launches live peril dashboard for commercial insurance
item.description: Fuse International, the AI-powered commercial insurance intelligence platform, has announced the general availability of Watch, a new product that plots every active US peril on a single live map, purpose-built for commercial insurance brokers and underwriters. According to Fuse, watch is organised around four views: a Peril Map plotting active events across the continental US, a Live Feed of insurance-relevant alerts with line-of-business context, a Forecast covering the next three days of severe-weather risk, and Market Pulse, a live read on the macro and commodity signals driving insurance pricing. It’s a live peril dashboard every commercial underwriter and every broker should have open on a second monitor.
